title: GPU, CUDA, and PyTorch Performance Optimizations (Create With)
description: Recurring monthly workshop by Chris Fregly and Antje Barth covering GPU architecture, CUDA programming, PyTorch optimization, profiling, mixed-precision training, memory management, and custom CUDA kernels.

title: PyTorch for Deep Learning Professional Certificate (DeepLearning.AI)
description: 87-hour comprehensive course covering tensors, neural networks, TorchVision, HuggingFace Transformers, Siamese networks, ResNets, deployment with ONNX and MLflow, and pruning/quantization.

title: Training PyTorch 2026: Optimizing AI Models in Production (Learni)
description: 1-day intensive covering PyTorch 2026 new features, torch.compile/Dynamo optimizations, distributed training with FSDP, MLOps with MLflow and Kubeflow, and enterprise deployment.

title: GPU Programming using CUDA (HLRS Stuttgart)
description: 7-day course covering CUDA programming model, memory management, GPU architecture, shared/constant memory, streams, multi-GPU programming, profiling, and CUDA libraries.

title: CUDA C++ Programming Guide (NVIDIA Official)
description: The definitive reference for CUDA programming: programming model, memory hierarchy, atomics, warp-level primitives, streams and events, dynamic parallelism, and cooperative groups.

title: NVIDIA Deep Learning Institute Courses
description: Official NVIDIA DLI courses covering fundamentals through advanced: Getting Started with GPUs, Accelerating CUDA C++ Applications, Optimizing Deep Learning with TensorRT, and Multi-GPU programming.

title: PyTorch Performance Tuning Guide (Official)
description: Official PyTorch performance guide covering torch.compile, data loading optimization, memory management, gradient checkpointing, mixed precision, distributed training, and profiling with torch.profiler.

title: Mixed Precision Training Setup with PyTorch AMP
description: Step-by-step workflow for enabling automatic mixed precision training to cut memory usage by 50% and speed up training by 2-3x.
workflow: Import torch.cuda.amp: from torch.cuda.amp import autocast, GradScaler
Create GradScaler: scaler = GradScaler()
Wrap forward pass with autocast: with autocast(): output = model(input)
Scale loss before backward: scaler.scale(loss).backward()
Update weights with scaler: scaler.step(optimizer); scaler.update()
Monitor for NaN/Inf: check scaler.get_scale() for sudden drops
Adjust learning rate: AMP typically needs 2-4x higher LR
Profile with torch.profiler to verify GPU utilization is high
Benchmark: compare throughput (samples/sec) and memory usage vs FP32

title: Distributed Training with PyTorch FSDP2
description: Set up Fully Sharded Data Parallel training for models that do not fit on a single GPU.
workflow: Install: pip install torch>=2.6 (FSDP2 requires PyTorch 2.6+)
Wrap model with FSDP: from torch.distributed.fsdp import FSDP; model = FSDP(model, auto_wrap_policy=...)
Configure sharding strategy: FULL_SHARD for maximum memory savings
Set up distributed sampler: DistributedSampler(dataset, num_gpus, rank)
Launch with torchrun: torchrun --nproc_per_node=4 train.py
Monitor GPU memory: each GPU holds 1/N of model parameters + gradients
Configure checkpointing: model.apply activation_checkpointing for large models
Profile cross-GPU communication with torch.profiler
Tune batch size: increase per-GPU batch to keep GPU utilization high
Validate: compare single-GPU accuracy with distributed accuracy

title: CUDA Kernel Profiling with Nsight
description: Profile CUDA kernels to identify bottlenecks and optimize GPU utilization.
workflow: Install NVIDIA Nsight Compute and Systems: apt install nsight-compute nsight-systems
Profile with Nsight Systems: nsys profile -o profile python train.py
Analyze timeline: nsys-ui profile.qdrep (visual timeline of kernels and memory transfers)
Profile specific kernel: ncu --set full -k my_kernel python train.py
Check key metrics: SM utilization, memory throughput, occupancy, warp execution efficiency
Identify bottlenecks: memory-bound (low SM utilization) vs compute-bound (high SM, low memory)
Optimize: increase block size, use shared memory, coalesce global memory access, minimize bank conflicts
Re-profile and compare: ncu --compare baseline.ncu-repo optimized.ncu-repo
Iterate until target utilization reached
Set up continuous profiling in CI: ncu --set basic python -m pytest tests/

title: RTX 5090 Optimization Checklist
description: Optimization checklist for the RTX 5090 Laptop GPU (24GB VRAM, Blackwell architecture).
workflow: Enable TF32 for matrix ops: torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True
Use bf16 mixed precision (native on RTX 5090): torch.autocast(device_type=cuda, dtype=torch.bfloat16)
Set CUDA benchmark: torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark = True
Configure memory: set PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF=max_split_size_mb:128
Use flash attention: install flash-attn for transformer models
Enable compile: model = torch.compile(model, mode=reduce-overhead)
Monitor VRAM: torch.cuda.memory_allocated() / 1024**3 for GB usage
Profile with torch.profiler: identify kernel bottlenecks
Use gradient checkpointing for large models: torch.utils.checkpoint.checkpoint
Batch size tuning: start with 2x FP32 batch size in AMP

title: Custom CUDA Kernel Development
description: Write and deploy a custom CUDA kernel using PyTorch CUDA extensions.
workflow: Create CUDA kernel file: kernel.cu with __global__ function
Write C++ wrapper: kernel.cpp using ATEN operators
Create setup.py with CUDAExtension builder
Build: pip install -e . (compiles CUDA kernel)
Import in Python: from my_kernels import fused_relu_add
Test correctness: compare against torch implementation with torch.testing.assert_close
Benchmark: compare torch.relu(x) + y vs fused_relu_add(x, y) with torch.utils.benchmark
Profile with Nsight Compute: verify kernel occupancy and memory throughput
Optimize: adjust block size, shared memory, warp-level primitives
Integrate into training loop: replace torch ops with fused kernels

title: PyTorch Mixed Precision Training with AMP
description: Complete mixed precision training script using PyTorch Automatic Mixed Precision.
code:
import torch
from torch.cuda.amp import autocast, GradScaler

model = MyModel().cuda()
optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=1e-3)
scaler = GradScaler()  # Handles FP16 gradient scaling

for epoch in range(num_epochs):
    for batch_idx, (data, target) in enumerate(train_loader):
        data, target = data.cuda(), target.cuda()
        optimizer.zero_grad()
        
        with autocast():  # Enable mixed precision
            output = model(data)
            loss = criterion(output, target)
        
        scaler.scale(loss).backward()  # Scale gradients
        scaler.step(optimizer)  # Update weights
        scaler.update()  # Update scale factor

        if batch_idx % 100 == 0:
            print(f"Epoch {epoch}, Batch {batch_idx}, Loss: {loss.item():.4f}")

title: PyTorch torch.compile Optimization
description: Using torch.compile for automatic kernel fusion and optimization.
code:
import torch

model = MyModel().cuda()

# Compile with different modes
compiled_model = torch.compile(model)  # Default: reduce-overhead
compiled_model = torch.compile(model, mode="reduce-overhead")  # Fastest for training
compiled_model = torch.compile(model, mode="default")  # Balanced
compiled_model = torch.compile(model, mode="max-autotune")  # Slowest compile, fastest runtime

# Use exactly like the original model
output = compiled_model(input_tensor)
loss = criterion(output, target)
loss.backward()  # Works with autograd

# Key benefits:
# - Automatic kernel fusion (combines multiple ops into one GPU kernel)
# - Reduced Python overhead (compiles to optimized GPU code)
# - Works with existing code (minimal changes needed)
# - First call is slower (compilation), subsequent calls are fast

title: GPU Memory Monitoring and OOM Prevention
description: Python utilities for monitoring GPU memory and preventing out-of-memory errors during training.
code:
import torch
import gc

def print_gpu_memory():
    """Print current GPU memory usage."""
    allocated = torch.cuda.memory_allocated() / 1024**3
    reserved = torch.cuda.memory_reserved() / 1024**3
    max_allocated = torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated() / 1024**3
    print(f"Allocated: {allocated:.2f} GB, Reserved: {reserved:.2f} GB, Peak: {max_allocated:.2f} GB")

def free_gpu_memory():
    """Force release unused GPU memory."""
    gc.collect()
    torch.cuda.empty_cache()
    torch.cuda.reset_peak_memory_stats()

# Prevent OOM with gradient checkpointing
from torch.utils.checkpoint import checkpoint

class CheckpointedModel(torch.nn.Module):
    def forward(self, x):
        # Use gradient checkpointing to trade compute for memory
        return checkpoint(self.expensive_layer, x)

# Set memory allocation config for fragmentation reduction
import os
os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"] = "max_split_size_mb:128"

title: Multi-GPU Data Parallel Training
description: PyTorch DistributedDataParallel setup for training across multiple GPUs.
code:
import torch
import torch.distributed as dist
from torch.nn.parallel import DistributedDataParallel as DDP

def setup_distributed():
    dist.init_process_group(backend="nccl")
    local_rank = int(os.environ["LOCAL_RANK"])
    torch.cuda.set_device(local_rank)
    return local_rank

def main():
    local_rank = setup_distributed()
    model = MyModel().cuda(local_rank)
    model = DDP(model, device_ids=[local_rank])
    optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=1e-3 * dist.get_world_size())
    
    sampler = torch.utils.data.distributed.DistributedSampler(dataset)
    loader = DataLoader(dataset, sampler=sampler, batch_size=32, num_workers=4)
    
    for epoch in range(num_epochs):
        sampler.set_epoch(epoch)
        for batch in loader:
            optimizer.zero_grad()
            loss = model(batch.cuda(local_rank))
            loss.backward()
            optimizer.step()

# Launch: torchrun --nproc_per_node=4 train.py

title: NVIDIA Nsight Systems
description: GPU profiling tool for visualizing application timeline, kernel execution, memory transfers, and API calls. Essential for identifying bottlenecks and optimizing GPU utilization.

title: NVIDIA Nsight Compute
description: Detailed kernel-level profiling tool that reports occupancy, memory throughput, compute throughput, and stall reasons for individual CUDA kernels.

title: torch.compile (PyTorch)
description: PyTorch 2.x JIT compilation system that automatically fuses operations, optimizes memory layout, and generates efficient GPU kernels. Works with minimal code changes: model = torch.compile(model).

title: NVIDIA TensorRT
description: High-performance deep learning inference optimizer and runtime. Compiles models to optimized TensorRT engines with layer/tensor fusion, kernel auto-tuning, and precision calibration for maximum throughput.

title: CUDA Toolkit
description: Complete development environment for GPU computing: nvcc compiler, cuBLAS, cuDNN, cuFFT, cuRAND, cuSPARSE, NPP, and Thrust. Required for custom CUDA kernel development.

title: Flash Attention Pattern
description: Memory-efficient attention that computes attention in tiles to avoid materializing the full NxN attention matrix. Reduces memory from O(N^2) to O(N) and is 2-4x faster. Available in PyTorch 2.x as torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention.

title: Gradient Checkpointing Pattern
description: Trade compute for memory by recomputing intermediate activations during backward pass instead of storing them. Reduces memory usage by 60-70% with 20-30% slower training. Use torch.utils.checkpoint.checkpoint() for large models.

title: Tensor Core Optimization Pattern
description: Maximize Tensor Core utilization on NVIDIA GPUs by using FP16/BF16 data types, aligning matrix dimensions to multiples of 8 (or 16 for FP8), and enabling TF32 for FP32 workloads.

title: Gradient Accumulation Pattern
description: Simulate larger batch sizes by accumulating gradients over multiple forward/backward passes before optimizer.step(). Essential when GPU memory limits batch size.

title: Asynchronous Data Loading Pattern
description: Overlap data loading and preprocessing with GPU computation using DataLoader with num_workers > 0 and pin_memory=True. Reduces GPU idle time and improves throughput by 30-50%.

title: PyTorch Performance Tuning Guide
description: Official guide covering torch.compile, data loading optimization, memory management, mixed precision, distributed training, and profiling with torch.profiler.

title: CUDA Programming Tutorial (NVIDIA)
description: Official CUDA programming tutorial series covering basics through advanced: thread hierarchy, memory hierarchy, synchronization, atomics, and streams.

title: PyTorch FSDP Tutorial
description: Official tutorial for Fully Sharded Data Parallel training covering model sharding, mixed precision, gradient checkpointing, and multi-node configuration.

title: Flash Attention Implementation Guide
description: How to use flash-attn in PyTorch: installation, usage with torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention, and integration with HuggingFace Transformers.

title: Programming Massively Parallel Processors (Kirk and Hwu)
description: The definitive textbook on GPU computing covering CUDA architecture, memory hierarchy, thread scheduling, reduction patterns, scan algorithms, and application case studies.

title: Deep Learning (Goodfellow, Bengio, Courville)
description: The canonical deep learning textbook covering mathematical foundations, optimization, CNNs, RNNs, autoencoders, generative models, and theoretical aspects. Free online.

title: CUDA Parallel Programming (Coursera/NVIDIA)
description: Official NVIDIA course covering CUDA thread hierarchy, memory types, synchronization patterns, and parallel algorithm implementation. Hands-on assignments with real GPU code.

title: Practical Deep Learning for Coders (fast.ai)
description: Jeremy Howard practical course using PyTorch and fastai library. Covers CNNs, NLP, collaborative filtering, tabular models, and deployment with a top-down approach.

title: Distributed Deep Learning with DeepSpeed
description: Microsoft DeepSpeed course covering ZeRO optimization, model parallelism, pipeline parallelism, mixed precision, and training models with trillions of parameters across GPU clusters.

title: DeepSpeed ZeRO-3 Training Setup
description: Configure DeepSpeed ZeRO-3 for training models that exceed single-GPU memory.
workflow: Install DeepSpeed: pip install deepspeed
Create deepspeed_config.json with ZeRO-3 configuration
Set zero_optimization.stage to 3 (full model sharding)
Configure offload: offload_optimizer and offload_param to CPU/NVMe
Set gradient_accumulation_steps for effective batch size
Launch with deepspeed launcher: deepspeed --num_gpus=4 train.py
Monitor GPU/CPU memory usage with nvidia-smi and htop
Adjust micro_batch_size to maximize GPU utilization
Enable activation checkpointing for further memory savings
Profile with DeepSpeed timeline: --deepspeed_timeline ds_timeline.json

title: Benchmark GPU Performance with PyTorch Profiler
description: Systematic approach to profiling GPU workloads and identifying bottlenecks.
workflow: Install profiler: pip install torch-tb-profiler
Add profiler context manager around training loop
Profile CPU and GPU activities: schedule=torch.profiler.schedule(wait=1, warmup=1, active=3)
Record shapes and memory: with_stack=True, profile_memory=True
Export to TensorBoard: profiler.export_chrome_trace('trace.json')
Analyze in Chrome Trace Viewer or TensorBoard
Check key metrics: GPU utilization, kernel time, memory transfers
Identify bottlenecks: low GPU utilization = CPU-bound, high memcpy = data-bound
Optimize based on findings: increase batch size, use pinned memory, add num_workers
Re-profile and compare with baseline

title: DDP with Gradient Accumulation and Mixed Precision
description: Production-ready DDP training script with gradient accumulation and mixed precision.
code:
import torch
import torch.distributed as dist
from torch.nn.parallel import DistributedDataParallel as DDP
from torch.cuda.amp import autocast, GradScaler

def train():
    dist.init_process_group(backend='nccl')
    local_rank = int(os.environ['LOCAL_RANK'])
    torch.cuda.set_device(local_rank)
    model = DDP(MyModel().cuda(local_rank), device_ids=[local_rank])
    optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=1e-4)
    scaler = GradScaler()
    accum_steps = 4  # effective batch = batch * accum * world_size
    for step, (data, target) in enumerate(train_loader):
        data, target = data.cuda(local_rank), target.cuda(local_rank)
        with autocast():
            loss = model(data, target) / accum_steps
        scaler.scale(loss).backward()
        if (step + 1) % accum_steps == 0:
            scaler.step(optimizer)
            scaler.update()
            optimizer.zero_grad()
# torchrun --nproc_per_node=4 train.py

title: DeepSpeed
description: Microsoft distributed training library with ZeRO (Zero Redundancy Optimizer) for training models with billions/trillions of parameters. Supports ZeRO-1/2/3, offloading, and pipeline parallelism.

title: PyTorch Profiler
description: Built-in PyTorch profiler for CPU/GPU workload analysis. Generates Chrome traces, TensorBoard visualizations, and memory timelines. Essential for identifying bottlenecks.

title: Weights and Biases (W&B)
description: Experiment tracking platform for ML: hyperparameter sweeps, metric logging, artifact versioning, and model registry. Integrates with PyTorch, HuggingFace, and DeepSpeed.

title: MLflow
description: Open-source platform for ML lifecycle management: experiment tracking, model registry, model serving, and deployment. Supports PyTorch, TensorFlow, and HuggingFace models.

title: Activation Checkpointing Pattern
description: Reduce memory by 60-70% by recomputing activations during backward pass instead of storing them. Use torch.utils.checkpoint.checkpoint() wrapper around expensive layers. Trade 20-30% more compute for 2-3x larger models on the same GPU.

title: Effective Batch Size Tuning Pattern
description: Simulate large batch sizes using gradient accumulation: effective_batch = micro_batch * accum_steps * num_gpus. Start with micro_batch that fills 80% GPU memory, then increase accum_steps until effective batch is 256-2048.

title: Learning Rate Warmup and Decay Pattern
description: Start with 1/10th of peak learning rate, linearly warm up over first 500-2000 steps, then decay with cosine schedule. Essential for stable training of large models. AdamW with warmup+cosine is the default for most LLM training.

title: DeepSpeed Getting Started Tutorial
description: Official DeepSpeed tutorial covering installation, ZeRO configuration, mixed precision training, and launching distributed training jobs.

title: PyTorch Distributed Data Parallel Tutorial
description: Official PyTorch DDP tutorial covering single-machine and multi-machine training, gradient synchronization, and debugging distributed training issues.

title: Oxford CUDA Programming on NVIDIA GPUs
description: One-week intensive at Oxford Mathematical Institute covering CUDA fundamentals, memory types, warp shuffles, reduction/scan, Tensor Cores, multi-GPU, streams, auto-tuning. 3hrs lectures + 4hrs practicals per day.

title: Fundamentals of Accelerated Computing with Modern CUDA C++ (NHR@FAU/NVIDIA DLI)
description: 3-day online course by NVIDIA DLI Ambassador covering CUDA C++ fundamentals, kernel launches, memory management, profiling, GPU architecture, performance optimization, and modern C++ with GPUs.

title: Fundamentals of Accelerated Computing with CUDA Python (NHR@FAU/NVIDIA DLI)
description: Online course covering CUDA Python with CuPy, cuDF, CCCL, kernel authoring, and memory spaces. Designed for Python-first GPU computing.

title: NVIDIA Accelerated Python Tutorial (GitHub)
description: Self-paced tutorial covering CUDA Python (CuPy, cuDF, CCCL, Kernels - 8hrs), CUDA core and CCCL (2hrs), and PyHPC (NumPy, CuPy, mpi4py - 4hrs). Run on NVIDIA Brev, Google Colab, or local Docker.

title: Training and Deploying Large-Scale Models (University Course)
description: 8-session university course covering distributed LLM training, multi-GPU parallelization (pipeline, tensor, data), communication-efficient optimization, post-training with TorchTune, serving with vLLM, and agentic AI. Uses torchtitan, torchft, torchtune.

title: Distributed Data Parallel in PyTorch Tutorial Series (Learncafe)
description: Free 2-hour video tutorial series covering DDP fundamentals, multi-GPU training with DDP, Torchrun, multinode training, and training a GPT-like model with DDP. Includes certificate of completion.
